title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1 – 6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues, a man and a woman, talking about a recent teamwork exercise.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Well, that 'escape room' challenge was certainly... intense. Woman: [1]I think it revealed quite a bit about our internal hierarchy, didn't it?[/1] Sarah took charge immediately, even though it wasn't her project. Man: It was fascinating to watch. I found myself taking a back seat because there were just too many voices in the room. Woman: [2]The facilitators said the goal was to improve communication, but I felt it actually highlighted our inability to listen[/2]. Man: True. Everyone was shouting their own solutions rather than building on others' ideas. Woman: Still, we did get out with five minutes to spare. Man: Just barely!

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Well, that 'escape room' challenge was certainly... intense.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I think it revealed quite a bit about our internal hierarchy, didn't it? Sarah took charge immediately, even though it wasn't her project.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It was fascinating to watch. I found myself taking a back seat because there were just too many voices in the room.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The facilitators said the goal was to improve communication, but I felt it actually highlighted our inability to listen.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True. Everyone was shouting their own solutions rather than building on others' ideas.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Still, we did get out with five minutes to spare.

questions.question: What did the woman notice about her team during the exercise?

options.A: They were surprisingly efficient under pressure.
options.B: Natural leadership roles were quickly established.
options.C: Some members were too intimidated to participate.

questions.explanation: She mentions that a colleague 'took charge immediately', revealing insights into their 'internal hierarchy'.

questions.question: How does the woman feel about the outcome of the exercise?

options.A: It successfully met its stated objectives.
options.B: It proved that the team works well together.
options.C: It exposed a fundamental flaw in the team's dynamics.

questions.explanation: She says it 'highlighted our inability to listen', which is a fundamental communication flaw.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new city bypass that has just opened.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Have you tried the new bypass yet? It saved me twenty minutes on my commute this morning. Woman: I have, but [3]I’m worried it’s a classic case of 'induced demand'[/3]. Within a year, it’ll be just as congested as the old road. Man: You’re such a cynic! Surely adding more lanes is a good thing? Woman: Not if it just encourages more people to drive instead of taking the train. [4]The focus should be on sustainable transport, not making it easier for cars[/4]. Man: But for people who have to drive, like me, it’s a lifesaver. Woman: For now, maybe. But wait until the new housing estates are built along the route. Then we'll see how fast your commute is.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Have you tried the new bypass yet? It saved me twenty minutes on my commute this morning.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I have, but I’m worried it’s a classic case of 'induced demand'. Within a year, it’ll be just as congested as the old road.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You’re such a cynic! Surely adding more lanes is a good thing?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not if it just encourages more people to drive instead of taking the train. The focus should be on sustainable transport, not making it easier for cars.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: But for people who have to drive, like me, it’s a lifesaver.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: For now, maybe. But wait until the new housing estates are built along the route. Then we'll see how fast your commute is.

questions.question: The woman predicts that the new road will...

options.A: be poorly maintained by the council.
options.B: fail to solve the long-term traffic problem.
options.C: cause an increase in local road accidents.

questions.explanation: She mentions 'induced demand' and predicts it will be 'just as congested' in a year.

questions.question: The woman's main argument is that urban planning should prioritize...

options.B: the development of new residential areas.

questions.explanation: She says the focus should be on 'sustainable transport' rather than making it 'easier for cars'.

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a recent change in a popular social media platform's algorithm.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Is it just me, or is my feed suddenly full of people I don't even follow? Woman: It’s the new update. [5]They’re prioritising 'discovery' over your actual social circle now[/5]. Man: I hate it. It feels like I’m being shouted at by strangers. Woman: [6]It’s clearly an attempt to compete with the short-form video apps[/6] that use the same model. They’re desperate to keep our 'screen time' up. Man: But the reason I used the app was to see what my friends were up to! Woman: I’m afraid the 'social' part of social media is becoming secondary to the 'media' part. It’s all about the content loop now.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Is it just me, or is my feed suddenly full of people I don't even follow?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s the new update. They’re prioritising 'discovery' over your actual social circle now.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I hate it. It feels like I’m being shouted at by strangers.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s clearly an attempt to compete with the short-form video apps that use the same model. They’re desperate to keep our 'screen time' up.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: But the reason I used the app was to see what my friends were up to!

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’m afraid the 'social' part of social media is becoming secondary to the 'media' part. It’s all about the content loop now.

questions.question: According to the woman, what is the platform's new focus?

options.A: Improving the privacy of its users.
options.C: Reducing the amount of advertising in the feed.

questions.explanation: She says they are prioritising 'discovery' (finding new things) over the 'actual social circle'.

questions.question: The woman believes the change was motivated by...

options.A: a desire to support independent creators.
options.C: intense competition from other tech companies.

questions.explanation: She says it's an 'attempt to compete with short-form video apps'.
